01 / Profile

The surgeon-engineer.

I'm a Consultant Oral & Maxillofacial Surgeon and doctoral researcher at Malmö University, practising a digital-first approach — every complex case is planned in Virtual Surgical Planning (VSP) and executed through CAD/CAM workflows.

I design and produce patient-specific implants in-house — selective-laser-melted titanium and PEEK, subperiosteal implants, and total TMJ prostheses — alongside microvascular free-flap and cleft & craniofacial reconstruction, across the full spectrum of maxillofacial surgery.

I also build the tools. I co-developed an internationally adopted open-source surgical-planning platform, putting tertiary-level reconstruction within reach of a government hospital outside a tertiary centre — at a fraction of commercial cost.

I'm a certified postgraduate Trainer and Examiner in OMFS at the Post Graduate Institute of Medicine (PGIM), Colombo, and Key Opinion Leader for Dentium in Sri Lanka.

Clinical Focus

  • Patient-specific PEEK & titanium implants
  • Subperiosteal implants & total TMJ replacement
  • Microvascular free-flap reconstruction
  • Head & neck oncology & reconstruction
  • Cleft lip & palate · craniofacial
  • Orthognathic (corrective-jaw) surgery
  • Virtual surgical planning & CAD/CAM
02 / Open source · VSP

Surgical technology, made open.

Affordable, transparent, reproducible planning — so advanced reconstruction isn't limited by where a patient is treated.

Planner · live

SlicerBoneReconstructionPlanner

Mandibular reconstruction with a vascularised fibula free flap, planned end-to-end in the open-source 3D Slicer extension — automated fibula segmentation, virtual osteotomies and printable resection & cutting guides.

~LKR 1.5k
Per-case guide cost
vs ~USD 8,000 commercial

Open hardware · Contributor

LibreRespire Ventilator

An open-source initiative developing an affordable, medical-grade mechanical ventilator in response to the COVID-19 shortage.

Visit project ↗
In-house · CAD / CAM

Patient-Specific Implant Workflow

End-to-end design and manufacture of titanium (SLM) and PEEK implants and resin surgical guides — print-failure held below 0.5% per resin bottle.
